_comment: Feature decomposition for prod-saas mission. Each feature's fulfills[] claims specific assertion IDs from validation-contract.md. Coverage invariant: every assertion claimed by exactly one feature.

features.description: Foundation for the entire mission's testing. Install @vitest/coverage-v8 with 80% line threshold on src/lib/**. Install @playwright/test with chromium, config at playwright.config.ts pointing webServer at `pnpm dev` on port 3000. Add Supabase CLI test harness: src/__tests__/helpers/supabase.ts with createTestUser(email) returning {userId, jwt}, clientAs(jwt), and truncateUserData() using service role. Wire setupFiles in vitest.config.ts with beforeEach truncation. Add `pnpm test:e2e` and `pnpm test:coverage` scripts to package.json. Add CI skeleton at .github/workflows/test.yml with postgres service container, lint + typecheck + vitest + playwright jobs, codecov upload. Install stripe-mock docker image reference in services.yaml (used in M2). Install fast-check and pdf-lib for adversarial tests (used in M3 and M5).
expectedBehavior: pnpm test:coverage produces coverage/ report with line % per file
pnpm test:e2e runs playwright against localhost:3000
src/__tests__/helpers/supabase.ts exports createTestUser/clientAs/truncateUserData
vitest.config.ts has setupFiles and 80% threshold on src/lib/**
.github/workflows/test.yml runs on push and PR
stripe-mock, fast-check, pdf-lib, @playwright/test, @vitest/coverage-v8 in devDependencies
verificationSteps: pnpm test:coverage and verify coverage/ output
pnpm test:e2e and verify a trivial spec runs (write e2e/smoke.spec.ts)
grep setupFiles vitest.config.ts
GitHub Actions workflow shown in Actions tab after push

features.description: Integration test suite proving cross-user data isolation on every user-scoped table. Uses createTestUser helper from add-test-infrastructure to spin up two users A and B per test, writes data as A with service role, then asserts B cannot SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE A's rows via their anon JWT. Covers: sites, pages, embeddings, leads, conversations. Also tests service-role intentional bypass (for webhooks) and anonymous (no-JWT, no-site_key) returning zero rows. Adversarial: widget with wrong site_key returns zero rows and does not leak schema errors. Stress: 50 parallel cross-user reads never cross-pollute.
expectedBehavior: src/__tests__/rls-sites.test.ts, rls-embeddings.test.ts, rls-pages.test.ts, rls-leads.test.ts, rls-conversations.test.ts each assert user A/B isolation for SELECT
src/__tests__/rls-mutations.test.ts asserts UPDATE and DELETE blocked cross-user
src/__tests__/rls-widget.test.ts asserts widget site_key scoping and spoofing rejection
src/__tests__/rls-bypass.test.ts asserts service-role reads all rows (intentional)
src/__tests__/rls-anonymous.test.ts asserts no-JWT reads return zero
src/__tests__/rls-concurrent.test.ts runs 50 parallel alternating reads with no cross-pollution
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/rls-*.test.ts and observe all pass
Inspect test output: row counts match expectations per assertion

features.description: Add POST /api/sites/rotate-key endpoint. Authenticated (requires user session). Generates new site_key via `encode(gen_random_bytes(16), 'hex')` (matches existing default), updates sites.site_key for the caller's site, returns the new key. Old key immediately invalid — next widget call with old key returns 401 from chat/session because site lookup fails. Add tests: happy path (new key returned, old key fails), auth required (401 without session), rate-limit (5/hour per user).
expectedBehavior: POST /api/sites/rotate-key returns 200 with {site_key} when authenticated
POST without auth returns 401
Old site_key invalid immediately after rotation (widget chat/session returns 401)
Rate limited to 5 rotations per user per hour
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/site-key-rotation.test.ts
curl rotation twice; verify second returns 429 or succeeds depending on rate window

features.description: Re-validate the 7 blocked + 1 failed auth assertions deferred from foundation round 1. Root fix: the /auth/callback route currently lands some magic-link flows on /login?error=auth_callback_error (VAL-AUTH-003 failure). Investigate: likely handling code-query params but not fragment tokens from Supabase Admin-generated links. Fix so both code-query and fragment-token flows reach /dashboard with valid sb-* cookies. Add src/__tests__/helpers/auth.ts with createTestSessionViaAdmin() that uses supabase.auth.admin.generateLink + direct token exchange, bypassing the OTP rate limit that blocked the original validation run. Update /auth/callback to preserve the originally-requested protected path (deep-link redirect). Assert callback rejects external-origin `next` params (open redirect). Assert sign-out clears sb-* cookies and magic links are single-use.
expectedBehavior: Visiting a valid magic link (code-query OR fragment-token form) lands on /dashboard with sb-* cookies present
Login form shows identical 'Check your email' for known and unknown emails (no enumeration)
/auth/callback strips external-origin next params, redirects to /dashboard fallback
Authenticated session persists across page refresh (cookies unchanged)
Sign-out POST /api/auth/signout clears sb-* cookies and redirects
After sign-out, all protected routes redirect to /login
Magic link is single-use: second exchange returns error
src/__tests__/helpers/auth.ts exports createTestSessionViaAdmin()
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/auth-*.test.ts
Playwright: e2e/auth-flow.spec.ts walks through login, refresh, signout, post-signout navigation

features.description: Defense-in-depth audit of secret handling. Confirm .env.local is in .gitignore (already done) and has never been committed (verified via git log -- .env.local). Ensure .env.example exists at repo root with every required var listed and commented (no real values). Update .env.example with vars we'll add in M2 (Stripe) and M8 (Upstash, Resend, Sentry) marked as 'Required from M2' / 'Required from M8'. Add a pre-commit hook (husky or simple shell) that scans staged files for patterns matching sk_live_*, sk_test_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}, whsec_*, and aborts commit if found. Document in README the rule: production secrets only in Vercel dashboard.
expectedBehavior: .env.example covers all current + M2 + M8 env vars with comments
Pre-commit hook aborts commit when staged diff contains secret-like patterns
README has a Secrets section naming Vercel as the production secret store
git log -- .env.local returns empty (confirms never committed)
verificationSteps: Attempt commit with a fake sk_test_abc123... string; verify hook aborts
Diff .env.example vs services.yaml env_requirements — all vars present

features.description: Create supabase/migrations/<ts>_plans.sql adding: (1) plans table (id text PK, stripe_price_id, monthly_message_limit, monthly_crawl_page_limit, supplementary_file_limit, display_name, price_cents); (2) extend profiles with plan_id text FK -> plans(id), current_period_start, current_period_end; (3) seed three plan rows (starter 2499/2000/500/25, pro 4999/7500/1500/100, scale 9900/25000/5000/500). stripe_price_id populated via one-shot script scripts/create-stripe-prices.mjs that calls Stripe API (test mode) and writes price IDs back via UPSERT. RLS: plans readable by anyone (public plan list), only service-role writes. Add tests asserting seed rows exist and plan caps match the mission.md pricing table.
expectedBehavior: supabase migration up succeeds and creates plans table + profiles additions
SELECT * FROM plans returns 3 rows with caps matching mission.md
scripts/create-stripe-prices.mjs populates stripe_price_id for each plan
RLS: anon can SELECT plans, cannot INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
src/__tests__/plans-schema.test.ts asserts seed data integrity
verificationSteps: supabase db reset && verify tables exist via psql
node scripts/create-stripe-prices.mjs (test mode); verify DB rows have price IDs

features.description: POST /api/stripe/checkout route. Body: {plan_id}. Auth required (401 without session). Validates plan_id against plans table (400 if unknown). Lazy-creates stripe customer (profiles.stripe_customer_id) if missing. Creates Stripe Checkout Session with mode='subscription', line_items from plan.stripe_price_id, success_url and cancel_url pointing to /dashboard/billing. Returns {url: session.url}. Adds src/lib/stripe/client.ts with memoized Stripe SDK instance (uses host=localhost:12111 when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY starts with 'sk_test_mock' for stripe-mock in tests). Tests cover: 200 happy path, 400 invalid plan_id, 401 no auth, lazy customer creation side-effect, correct success/cancel URLs.
expectedBehavior: POST /api/stripe/checkout with {plan_id:'starter'} + auth returns 200 with {url}
POST with invalid plan_id returns 400
POST without auth returns 401
First call for a user creates stripe_customer and stores stripe_customer_id on profiles
Subsequent calls reuse the same stripe_customer_id
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/stripe-checkout.test.ts
Start stripe-mock, start dev server, curl with session cookie, verify 200 and URL

features.description: POST /api/stripe/webhook route. Reads raw body (required for signature verification — disable Next.js body parser for this route). Calls stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET). Returns 400 on invalid signature, 400 if event created > 5 min ago. Uses processed_stripe_events table for idempotency: INSERT event_id UNIQUE first, if conflict return 200 without reprocessing. Handles event types: checkout.session.completed (set stripe_subscription_id + plan_id + status + period dates), customer.subscription.updated (sync), customer.subscription.deleted (status='canceled'), invoice.paid (reset usage_counters for period), invoice.payment_failed (status='past_due'). All events 200 after processing. Uses service-role client (no user JWT). Structured logging per event.
expectedBehavior: Valid signature + fresh timestamp + new event_id -> 200 and DB updated
Invalid signature -> 400, no DB change
Expired timestamp (> 5 min) -> 400
Duplicate event_id -> 200 but no double-processing
checkout.session.completed writes stripe_subscription_id, plan_id, current_period_end
customer.subscription.updated syncs fields including status transitions
customer.subscription.deleted sets status='canceled'
invoice.paid resets messages_used and crawl_pages_used
invoice.payment_failed sets status='past_due'
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/stripe-webhook*.test.ts (signature, idempotency, each event type)
stripe trigger checkout.session.completed via CLI against local webhook

features.description: POST /api/stripe/portal route. Auth required. Looks up profiles.stripe_customer_id for caller; if null returns 400 (no subscription yet). Calls stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create({customer, return_url: `${APP_URL}/dashboard/billing`}). Returns {url}. Client redirects. Tests: happy path 200 with URL, 401 without auth, 400 without subscription.
expectedBehavior: Authenticated POST with existing stripe_customer_id returns 200 with billing portal URL
Authenticated POST without stripe_customer_id returns 400
POST without auth returns 401
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/stripe-portal.test.ts

features.description: Replace src/lib/subscription.ts stub. New checkSubscription(userId) reads profiles.{subscription_status, trial_ends_at, current_period_end}: returns {active: true} when status in ('trialing','active') AND relevant date in the future; returns {active: false, reason, upgrade_url: '/dashboard/billing'} otherwise. Wire into /api/chat/session and /api/crawl/start: on !active, return 402 with {error: 'subscription_inactive', upgrade_url}. Tests cover each status + time combination: trialing + trial_ends_at future = active; trialing + expired = inactive; active + period future = active; past_due = inactive; canceled = inactive; canceled + period future (still paid through period) = active. Widget path handling for 402: widget renders a generic 'Service temporarily unavailable' with no sensitive details.
expectedBehavior: trialing + trial_ends_at in future = active; can chat and crawl
trialing + trial_ends_at expired = inactive; 402 on chat and crawl
active + current_period_end in future = active
past_due = inactive; 402 response includes upgrade_url
canceled + current_period_end in future = active (paid-through grace)
canceled + current_period_end past = inactive
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/subscription-gate.test.ts
Manual curl: create test profile per each status, hit /api/chat/session, verify 200 vs 402

features.description: Rewrite /dashboard/billing per Appendix B.1 of tasks/todo.md. Server component fetches profile + plan + last-12 invoices (via stripe.invoices.list). Renders: heading, PlanCard (plan name, status pill, price, usage meters), TrialCountdown (if trialing), invoice list with Stripe-hosted PDF links, next-invoice date, and three action buttons: [Manage billing] -> POST /api/stripe/portal, [Upgrade] / [Downgrade] -> POST /api/stripe/checkout with target plan. Guest redirected to /login (via existing dashboard layout). Renders proration preview when clicking Upgrade (stripe.invoices.retrieveUpcoming). States: skeleton while loading; error banner on fetch failure; trial banner when trialing. Microcopy and visual style per Appendix A.
expectedBehavior: Guest GET /dashboard/billing -> 302 /login
Pre-subscription user sees plan options and Upgrade CTAs
Trialing user sees countdown banner 'Trial ends in N days'
Active user sees current plan, price, status pill, usage meters
Upgrade button creates proration-preview; confirming redirects to Stripe Checkout or portal
Downgrade similarly creates credit preview
Invoice list renders last 12 with PDF links
Next-invoice date displayed when on active subscription
verificationSteps: Playwright e2e/billing-page.spec.ts: walk through guest, trialing, active, upgrade, downgrade
Manual: visit /dashboard/billing in each state via seeded profiles

features.description: Create supabase/migrations/<ts>_usage_counters.sql: table usage_counters (user_id uuid PK FK profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, period_start timestamptz, period_end timestamptz, messages_used int default 0, crawl_pages_used int default 0, files_stored int default 0, openai_tokens_used bigint default 0). RLS: users SELECT own row only; writes service-role only. Add Postgres RPC increment_message_counter(user_id uuid, limit int) that atomically: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE, check messages_used < limit, if yes increment and return {ok:true, used, limit}, else return {ok:false, used, limit}. All in one transaction for race safety. Trigger on profiles insert creates the usage_counters row with period_start = now() and period_end = now() + '1 month'. Tests cover RLS isolation and RPC atomicity.
expectedBehavior: usage_counters table exists with correct columns and FK cascade
RLS: user A cannot SELECT user B's usage_counters row
Service role SELECT returns all rows
increment_message_counter RPC returns {ok, used, limit} atomically
New profile insert triggers a usage_counters row with period defaults
verificationSteps: supabase db reset and verify table + trigger + RPC via psql
pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/usage-counters-schema.test.ts

features.description: Integrate increment_message_counter RPC into /api/chat/session: before embedding/LLM, call the RPC with user's monthly_message_limit from their plan. If {ok:false}, return 402 with {error:'quota_exceeded', upgrade_url:'/dashboard/billing'} and do NOT make any OpenAI call. Widget handling: on 402 response, render a polite 'monthly chat limit reached' message (generic — no owner dashboard URL exposed to visitors). Adversarial test: fast-check property with 20 parallel requests at budget=10 asserts exactly 10 succeed. Idempotency: duplicate invoice.paid does not double-reset (covered in stripe-webhook-api but asserted here from quota side).
expectedBehavior: Each successful /api/chat/session increments messages_used by exactly 1
At messages_used >= limit, next call returns 402 with {error:'quota_exceeded', upgrade_url}
No OpenAI call made on 402 response
20 concurrent requests at budget=10 yield exactly 10 successes, 10 x 402
Widget on 402 renders generic 'limit reached' message (no owner-dashboard URL)
Duplicate invoice.paid event does not double-reset counters
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/quota-message-*.test.ts
Playwright: widget over-quota state screenshot

features.description: Gate /api/crawl/start with plan's monthly_crawl_page_limit. Before calling Firecrawl: SELECT usage_counters.crawl_pages_used for the user and plan.monthly_crawl_page_limit. If crawl_pages_used + estimated_pages_for_this_crawl > limit, return 402 with {error:'crawl_quota_exceeded', upgrade_url}. Firecrawl webhook processor increments crawl_pages_used by the actual indexed page count after completion (not estimate). This means a crawl is allowed if even partially within budget; if it grossly overshoots, the next crawl is blocked until reset.
expectedBehavior: Crawl start blocked with 402 when crawl_pages_used already >= limit
Firecrawl webhook processor increments crawl_pages_used by actual page count
No Firecrawl API call made on 402 rejection
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/quota-crawl.test.ts
Simulate webhook completion with 120 pages; verify counter += 120

features.description: Extend stripe-webhook-api's invoice.paid handler to reset usage_counters for the user: messages_used=0, crawl_pages_used=0, files_stored unchanged (storage persists across billing periods), period_start=invoice.period_start, period_end=invoice.period_end. Idempotent via processed_stripe_events. Tested separately from the webhook signature/event-routing tests so quota-specific behavior has its own coverage.
expectedBehavior: invoice.paid resets messages_used and crawl_pages_used to 0
period_start and period_end updated to invoice period
files_stored is NOT reset (storage is cumulative)
Duplicate event does not re-reset
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/quota-reset.test.ts

features.description: Build UsageMeterSet component per Appendix B.7. Renders N horizontal progress bars (label + current/max + bar). Mount on /dashboard overview and /dashboard/settings/billing. Data flow: server-side fetch of usage_counters + plan on initial render; client-side Supabase Realtime subscription on usage_counters for live updates. On upgrade, limits update without page reload (Realtime fires on profiles.plan_id change OR we revalidate via server action). Progress bar width capped at 100% even if overshoot. Empty state (new user, zero usage) shows 0/limit cleanly. Screenshot evidence for each state.
expectedBehavior: UsageMeterSet renders 3 bars (messages, crawl pages, files) with current/max
Bars on /dashboard and /dashboard/settings/billing match
Realtime: updating usage_counters in DB updates the meter within 3s
Upgrade changes the max values without a full reload
Overshoot (used > limit) caps bar width at 100%
Empty state (0 usage) renders cleanly
verificationSteps: Playwright: e2e/usage-meter.spec.ts covers render, live update, overshoot cap
Manual: open two tabs, send chat in one, watch the meter update in the other

features.description: Create src/app/dashboard/settings/layout.tsx with left sub-nav (Site, Knowledge, Responses, Escalation, Billing) per Appendix B.2. Uses usePathname to highlight active item using the app's existing active-state pattern from src/app/dashboard/sidebar.tsx. Main content area renders children. Parent /dashboard/settings page component redirects to /dashboard/settings/site. All settings sub-routes protected by parent dashboard layout auth check. Mobile viewport (< 640px): sidebar collapses into a horizontal tab strip above content, not stacked.
expectedBehavior: /dashboard/settings redirects to /dashboard/settings/site
Sidebar shows 5 items: Site, Knowledge, Responses, Escalation, Billing
Active item matches current pathname via distinct styling
Unauth'd access to any /dashboard/settings/* redirects to /login
Mobile viewport collapses sidebar cleanly (no awkward stacking)
verificationSteps: Playwright: e2e/settings-nav.spec.ts walks all 5 sub-routes
Playwright with mobile viewport: screenshot at 375px

features.description: Move existing src/app/dashboard/settings/settings-client.tsx logic into src/app/dashboard/settings/site/page.tsx (+ site-client.tsx). Preserves all current fields: site name, greeting message, Calendly URL, Google Maps URL. Save action unchanged. No functional regression. Old /dashboard/settings route now hits the new layout and redirects (via settings-layout feature).
expectedBehavior: /dashboard/settings/site renders existing settings form
Saving persists fields identically to pre-refactor behavior
No regressions in existing src/__tests__/dashboard-*.test.ts suites
verificationSteps: pnpm vitest run (all existing tests pass)
Playwright: e2e/settings-site.spec.ts submits form and reloads, verifies values

features.description: Add a 'Rotate site key' button in /dashboard/settings/site (below the existing fields). Click -> confirmation modal ('This will immediately invalidate the current site key. Your widget will need to be re-embedded.') -> POST /api/sites/rotate-key (endpoint from M1 feature site-key-rotation-api) -> toast 'Site key rotated' on success -> refresh the displayed key and update the embed snippet shown on /dashboard/embed. No page reload needed — uses server action or client-side refetch.
expectedBehavior: Rotate button opens confirmation modal with warning text
Confirming POSTs to /api/sites/rotate-key and displays the new key
Toast appears on success
Embed page (/dashboard/embed) reflects the new key without a full refresh
Old key immediately fails on widget calls (validated via M1 site-key-rotation-api's test)
verificationSteps: Playwright: e2e/site-key-rotate.spec.ts clicks rotate, asserts modal, toast, new key displayed

features.description: Install sonner. Add <Toaster /> once in src/app/layout.tsx or dashboard layout. Theme to match app (use --ink-primary / --bg-surface tokens from globals.css). Create src/lib/toast.ts thin wrapper exporting toast.success/error/loading with sensible defaults (4s duration, top-right position). Used by settings save, file upload, responses save, escalation save, billing actions.
expectedBehavior: sonner in dependencies; <Toaster /> mounted once in layout
toast.success('Saved') on settings save displays a toast
Toast auto-dismisses after 4s
Theme uses app color tokens (no jarring mismatch)
verificationSteps: Playwright: trigger a save action, screenshot with toast visible
Visual review: toast color scheme matches app
